issues.summary: Publish Website on Google APi->credentials>audience [PUBLISH]
Polish Logo
Get team to come up with logo candidates
Privacy Policy
issues.description:
Privacy Policy — Action Steps to Clear Google OAuth Verification
The Confluence privacy policy page has been fully drafted and is ready for review. Completing the steps below will unblock the Google OAuth verification, remove the 'unverified app' warning screen for new users, and display Vegangenius Chef branding on the Google Sign-In consent screen.
Confluence page: Privacy Policy for tasteslikegood.org
Steps
Step 1 — Review the Confluence page
Open the Confluence page linked above
Confirm all sections are accurate, especially Section 3.1 (OAuth scopes) and Section 5 (Google Limited Use statement)
Remove Section 15 (internal checklist) from the publicly hosted version
Step 2 — Host the policy publicly
Publish the policy at: https://tasteslikegood.org/privacy-policy
The page must be accessible without login
Verify it loads in an incognito window
Step 3 — Update Google Cloud Console
Go to Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen
Paste https://tasteslikegood.org/privacy-policy into the Privacy Policy URL field
Confirm app name, logo, and support email are filled in
Save changes
Step 4 — Submit for Google verification
On the OAuth consent screen page, click Submit for verification
Justification for generative-language.peruserquota scope: 'The app uses the user's Gemini API quota to generate vegan recipes on their behalf. No personal information is sent to the Gemini model — only the recipe prompt text entered by the user.'
Expected review time: 3–7 business days
Step 5 — Site footer and Terms of Service
Add Privacy Policy link to the site footer on all pages
Link to Privacy Policy from Terms of Service page (KAN-6)
Acceptance Criteria
Privacy policy hosted at public URL
URL entered in Google Cloud Console OAuth consent screen
Verification submitted to Google
Google consent screen shows Vegangenius Chef branding (not 'unverified app' warning)
Privacy policy linked in site footer and Terms of Service

issues.summary: "New Login" - "Authenticated Home View" not updating. Investigate cause and create PR with fix for Login bug that requires Users too "Click the login button after successful Auth0 w/ google in order for the view too show their 'Kitchen/Cookbooks Profile"
Add and enforce CI for Angular + Flask: builds, lint, and tests
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/104
Summary
Set up and enforce a CI pipeline that covers both the Angular frontend and Flask backend with automated builds, linting, and test runs on every PR and push to main.
Goals
Prevent broken builds from merging
Enforce code quality via lint gates
Surface test failures before review
Scope
Angular: ng build, ng lint, ng test (headless)
Flask: install deps, flake8/pylint, pytest
Run on: pull_request and push to main
Block merges on failure (branch protection rule)
Acceptance Criteria
GitHub Actions workflow runs on every PR
Build, lint, and test steps all present for both frontend and backend
PRs cannot merge if CI fails
Badge added to README showing CI status

issues.summary: Fix path injection vulnerabilities in recipe_repository.py and image_service.py
issues.description:
Summary
CodeQL (CVE: py/path-injection) flagged uncontrolled data used in path expressions in two Backend files. User-supplied filenames flow into file system operations without sufficient sanitization.
Affected locations
repositories/recipe_repository.py:88 — path expression using uncontrolled input
repositories/recipe_repository.py:157 — path expression using uncontrolled input
services/image_service.py:93 — path expression using uncontrolled input
services/image_service.py:131 — path expression using uncontrolled input
services/image_service.py:134 — path expression using uncontrolled input
Context
recipe_repository.py has validate_recipe_filepath() using os.path.abspath comparison for directory traversal prevention, but CodeQL still flags specific call sites as not properly guarded. image_service.py may be calling file operations with user-supplied paths before validation.
Acceptance Criteria
All 5 CodeQL py/path-injection alerts resolved or suppressed with justification
File path operations use validated, sanitized paths at every call site
validate_recipe_filepath() (or equivalent) is called before any open() on user-supplied paths
CodeQL scan passes clean on next PR

issues.summary: [GH#162] VALKEY_HOST singleton-guard: early return leaves orphaned client running
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/162

*Problem:*
In server/valkey.ts, createValkeyClient() returns null early when VALKEY_HOST is unset, even if a client was previously created with that variable set. This leaves the existing client (and any refresh timer) running, creating a resource leak.

The documented behavior says an existing healthy client should be returned immediately — the implementation contradicts this.

*Steps to reproduce:
1. Call createValkeyClient() with VALKEY_HOST set
2. Unset VALKEY_HOST
3. Call createValkeyClient() again — returns null but existing client still runs

*Fix options:
- Clean up the existing client before the early return (call shutdownValkey() first)
- OR update the docs to reflect actual behavior

*Priority:* P1 — labeled by original reviewer
*Affects:* Rate limiter distributed state across Express replicas

issues.summary: [GH#163] Catch quit() failures before reinitializing Valkey client
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/163

*Problem:*
In server/valkey.ts, when the singleton guard detects an unhealthy client, it calls shutdownValkey() which attempts client.quit(). If client.quit() fails (common with broken sockets), the rejection prevents reinitialization and recovery.

Result: transient Redis/Valkey failures escalate into hard failures instead of recovering gracefully.

*Expected behavior:
If client.quit() throws/rejects, the reinit should proceed anyway — the old broken connection should be discarded and a fresh client created.

*Fix:
Wrap client.quit() in try/catch (or .catch(() => {})) so that quit failures are swallowed and reinitialization continues.

*Priority:* P1 — flagged in code review
*Affects:* Rate limiter resilience; impacts  in legacy tracking

issues.summary: [GH#103] Pytest failing — Flask context error in generate_ai_image()
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegood.com/issues/103

*Repo:* Backend submodule (tasteslikegood.com)

*Problem:
Tests fail because generate_ai_image() attempts to access Flask request-bound objects (session, request context) without an active application context.

*Error: RuntimeError: Working outside of request context

*Root cause:
The function accesses Flask's session or request objects directly without first checking if a request context is active.

*Fix:
Guard session access with flask.has_request_context() checks, e.g.:
{{python
if flask.has_request_context():
    user_credentials = session.get('credentials')
else:
    user_credentials = None
}}

*Labels:* bug, python, CI/CD
*Affects:* CI pipeline — tests fail on PR, blocking merges

issues.summary: v0.2 Anti-Recipe Site Distribution Layer
issues.description:
h1. Vision: The Anti-Recipe Site
The objective of v0.2 is to shift focus to distribution by building a public, SSR-rendered, zero-fluff recipe site on top of our existing authenticated app. 
h3. Key Highlights
Speed as a feature: <500ms full render.
Honest URLs: /r/thai-peanut-noodles.
Visuals: One breathtaking AI-generated photo per recipe.
Conversion: Low-friction "Save to cookbook" CTA routing into the SPA.
SEO: Perfect Schema.org JSON-LD per recipe.
h3. Design Integration
The implementation must adhere strictly to the Claude Design handoff:
CSS Strategy: Combine React inline styles and colors_and_type.css into Backend/static/css/recipe-site.css.
Template Architecture: Break React components down into base.html, browse.html, and recipe.html Jinja files.
Design Decisions: Overlay hero image style and a 3-column responsive grid for the browse page.
Vanilla JS Conversion: Replace React hooks with Vanilla JavaScript for public SSR pages.
Mobile Responsiveness: Stack two-column layouts and ensure 44px touch targets below 768px.

issues.summary: v0.2 Data Model & Migrations
issues.description: Add is_public (Boolean, default False) and slug (String, unique, indexed) to Backend/models/recipe.py. Generate the associated Alembic migration script. Include a dedicated unit test for the upcoming backfill migration to ensure no downtime during deployment.

issues.summary: v0.2 Vanilla JS Interactions
issues.description: Convert the React state hooks from the prototype into lightweight Vanilla JavaScript for the SSR templates. This includes the "Save to Cookbook" toast notification animation and the "My Kitchen" SPA redirect modal logic. This ensures a fast <500ms render without heavy client-side frameworks on the public pages.

issues.summary: v0.2 SEO, JSON-LD & Distribution
issues.description: Create a dedicated RecipePresenter service to isolate SEO formatting from the route controllers. Use this to format Schema.org Recipe JSON-LD (e.g., ISO 8601 durations). Add canonical URLs, OG image tags (using AI image), Twitter Cards, and meta descriptions to recipe.html. Include a Pinterest share button and an auto-generated /sitemap.xml.

issues.summary: v0.2 SSR Routes & Express Proxy Config
issues.description: Implement Flask SSR routes: /r/<slug> for viewing a single public recipe and /browse for a paginated list of public recipes. Enforce eager loading (joinedload) on the /browse route to prevent N+1 queries. Modify image endpoints to allow public access for is_public=True. Update server/proxy.ts to correctly route /r/*, /browse, and /sitemap.xml to Flask before the Angular catch-all.

issues.summary: v0.2 Data Backfill & Integrity Retry Loop
issues.description: Write a script to backfill the slug field for the 85 existing legacy recipes based on their titles. Must implement a robust database retry loop that catches IntegrityError to safely append collision suffixes (-2, -3) and prevent concurrent race conditions on publish.

issues.summary: v0.2 Angular Editor Publish UI
issues.description: Update the recipe view/edit surface in the Angular SPA. Add a "Publish" toggle and a customizable "Slug" text input field, allowing owners to manage the public visibility of their recipes. Ensure loading, success, and error states are represented in the UI (e.g., spinner swap on publish, red border on failed slug).

issues.summary: v0.2 Jinja Templates & Design System
issues.description:
Create Backend/static/css/recipe-site.css by combining the Claude Design prototype's colors_and_type.css with its inline styles. Rewrite recipe.html, browse.html, and base.html in Jinja, consuming SQLAlchemy models. 
Implement the "Anti-AI Slop" design tokens (Playfair Display, true black/white, sharp borders) and mobile responsiveness (collapse grids <768px, 44px minimum touch targets).

issues.summary: Bug: UI shows logged out state (Login button) while still displaying authenticated recipes
issues.description:
When opening the website in a browser that was previously logged in, all recipes are shown as if the user is authenticated, but the login button still appears as if the user is logged out. This suggests a mismatch between the session state (or token presence) and the UI state of the authentication button.
Steps to reproduce:
Log into the application.
Close the browser/tab.
Re-open the application.
Observe that private/authenticated recipes are visible.
Observe that the UI still shows the 'Login' button instead of the authenticated state.

issues.summary: ** Avoid clearing cached session on transient auth-check failures**
issues.description:
Avoid clearing cached session on transient auth-check failures
checkAuthStatus() treats any non-OK response or fetch error as false, so this new call now runs not only for truly unauthenticated users but also during temporary /api/auth/check failures (e.g., backend restart, 500, network blip). In those cases clearStaleAuthenticatedSession() deletes vegan_genius_session, which can discard local state; that is especially risky because PersistenceService.saveRecipe() writes to local storage before a best-effort API sync, so unsynced edits can be lost. Please only clear cached authenticated state when the backend explicitly confirms authenticated: false, and preserve local state on transport/server errors.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
_Originally posted by @chatgpt-codex-connector[bot] in https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/pull/2860#discussion_r3111731885_
